about.title: About Root
about.intro: Root is Thailand's plant marketplace built on two simple ideas: every plant deserves a permanent identity, and every buyer deserves to know what a fair price looks like. We are not a rare-plant club. We are for every plant, common or rare.

origin.title: How We Started
origin.p1: In 2022, Thawanrat bought a Monstera Thai Constellation from a Facebook group for 18,000 THB. Six months later she sold it for 12,000. Was that fair? She had no idea — there was no price data, no market reference, just whatever the buyer was willing to pay.
origin.p2: Meanwhile, Pongsiri was tracking his plant collection in a spreadsheet, manually logging every purchase, every propagation, every sale. He dreamed of a system where each plant carried its own history — not in a spreadsheet, but physically, on the plant itself.
origin.p3: They met at a plant swap in Chatuchak and spent the next 18 months building Root. The QR provenance system was first: every plant gets a unique QR code at listing, and every ownership transfer is recorded permanently. Then came the price history graphs — inspired by Steam's marketplace — showing real transaction data so buyers and sellers could make informed decisions.
origin.p4: Root launched in Bangkok in early 2024. Within three months we had sellers in Chiang Mai, Phuket, Khon Kaen, and Udon Thani. Today we list plants at every price point, from seedlings to specimen plants. If it grows in Thailand, it has a home here.

transparency.title: Transparency First
transparency.desc: Every sale feeds into public price history. No hidden deals, no whisper networks. The market decides what a plant is worth.

provenance.title: Permanent Provenance
provenance.desc: Every plant gets a QR code at first listing. Scan it and see its entire ownership history — every seller, every price, forever.

data.title: Data for Everyone
data.desc: Before you buy, see 180 days of price history. Before you sell, see where your price sits against the market median.

escrow.title: Escrow Protection
escrow.desc: Pay via PromptPay, funds held in escrow until you confirm delivery with a QR scan. Sellers get paid, buyers get protection.

fee.title: 8% Flat Fee
fee.desc: No listing fees, no monthly subscriptions, no featured placement charges. We take 8% when you sell. That is it.

everyPlant.title: For Every Plant
everyPlant.desc: Every species, every price point. If it grows, it belongs here.

thawanrat.bio: Plant collector turned marketplace builder. Grew her first collector plant from a cutting in 2018.

james.bio: Fintech engineer and hydroponics enthusiast. Built the payments and provenance stack.

malai.bio: Manages seller onboarding and dispute resolution. 15 years in Thai e-commerce.

chanida.bio: Agricultural scientist ensuring species data accuracy across the platform.

cta.title: Join the marketplace
cta.description: Whether you are selling your first propagation or running a full nursery, Root gives you the tools to sell with transparency and confidence.

faq.title: Common Questions
faq.startSellingAnswer: Create an account, go to Seller Dashboard, and click "New Listing". You will need a PromptPay ID for payouts.
faq.damaged: What if my plant arrives damaged?
faq.damagedAnswer: Open a dispute within 48 hours of delivery. Upload photos and our team will review within 24 hours.
faq.qr: How does the QR provenance work?
faq.qrAnswer: When you list a plant, we generate a unique QR code. Attach it to the plant. Every future owner can scan it to see full history.
faq.commonPlantsAnswer: Absolutely. Root is for every plant, common or rare.
faq.paid: When do I get paid as a seller?
faq.paidAnswer: Funds are released 48 hours after delivery confirmation (or auto-confirmed). Payouts process within 1-2 business days.
faq.refundsAnswer: Refunds are handled through our dispute process. Open a dispute within 48 hours of delivery with photos and details. Our team will review and may issue a full or partial refund.
faq.disputesAnswer: Open a dispute from your order page within 48 hours of delivery. Upload clear photos and a description. We review evidence from both sides and aim to resolve within 24 hours.
faq.pickupSafetyAnswer: Meet in a public, well-lit place and confirm the plant matches the listing before handing over payment. Use ROOTS escrow whenever possible for extra protection.
faq.plantCondition: What condition should the plant arrive in?
faq.plantConditionAnswer: Plants should match the listing photos and description. Minor shipping stress is normal for living plants, but significant damage or misrepresentation can be disputed.
faq.shippingDamage: What if my plant is damaged in shipping?
faq.shippingDamageAnswer: Take photos of the packaging and plant immediately, then open a dispute within 48 hours. Sellers are responsible for safe packing; couriers are responsible for transit care.
faq.accountDeletion: How do I delete my account?
faq.accountDeletionAnswer: Email us from your registered address requesting account deletion. We will anonymise your data in provenance chains and remove personal information within 30 days, per Thai PDPA rules.
faq.reportScam: How do I report a scam?
faq.reportScamAnswer: Use the report button on any listing, message, or user profile, or email us with screenshots and details. We investigate quickly and may ban accounts that violate our terms.

fees.title: Simple, Transparent Fees
fees.subtitle: 8% from sellers only. No hidden costs, no monthly fees.

howItWorksPage.title: How Root Works
howItWorksPage.subtitle: Buy and sell plants the easy way. QR verification, escrow protection, and price history so you know you are getting a fair deal.

browse.title: 1. Browse & Buy
browse.desc: Explore rare plants from sellers across Thailand. Every listing shows a price history graph so you know if you're getting a fair deal. Filter by species, size, price, and location.

sellerQr.title: 2. Seller Lists with QR Tag
sellerQr.desc: When a seller creates a listing, we generate a unique QR code for that physical plant. The seller prints and attaches it to the pot. This QR becomes the plant's permanent digital identity.

escrow.title: 3. Secure Escrow Payment
escrow.desc: Pay via PromptPay or card. Your money is held in escrow — the seller only receives it after you confirm delivery. Platform fee is 8%, taken from the seller's side only.

ship.title: 4. Seller Ships with QR
ship.desc: The seller packs the plant with its QR tag attached and ships via your chosen courier. You'll receive a tracking number to monitor delivery.

scan.title: 5. You Scan to Confirm
scan.desc: When the plant arrives, scan the QR code with your phone to verify it's the same plant from the listing. Upload 1-3 receipt photos, then confirm delivery to release funds.

priceHistory.title: 6. Price History Updates
priceHistory.desc: Your purchase price is anonymously added to the species price history graph. Future buyers can see market trends, and you can track your plant's value over time.

cta.title: Ready to start?
cta.description: Create an account to browse, buy, sell, and start building your plant's provenance history.

provenance.title: What is Root Provenance?
provenance.description: A unique QR tag — a plant's digital birth certificate — that records its full history of owners and sales, so buyers can verify a seller's claims and rare plants keep their value across owners.

priceHistory.title: This Plant's Price History
priceHistory.note: Individual plant price history overlaid against species average

disputes.transitDamage: Transit damage claim — resolved for seller

collect.title: 1. Information We Collect
collect.intro: We collect the following information to operate Root Plant Market:

categories.accountData: Email, display name, password hash, profile photo (optional)
categories.shippingData: Name, address, district, province, postal code, phone number
categories.paymentData: PromptPay ID (phone or national ID number) — we do not store bank details
categories.listingsData: Photos, descriptions, pricing, plant species information
categories.transactionsData: Purchase history, sale history, dispute records
categories.usageData: Pages visited, searches made, device type, IP address

items.marketplace: To provide the marketplace service — connecting buyers and sellers
items.payments: To process payments via Omise PromptPay
items.qr: To generate QR provenance codes for plants
items.priceHistory: To calculate and display aggregate price history (anonymous)
items.notifications: To send order updates, payment confirmations, and platform notifications
items.fraud: To detect fraud, abuse, and market manipulation

share.title: 3. What We Share
share.intro: We do not sell your personal data. We only share:

items.shipping: Seller to buyer (post-payment): Your shipping address is shared with the seller only after you pay
items.contact: Buyer to seller (post-payment): Your display name and contact details are shared with the seller
items.omise: Omise: Payment processing data is shared with our payment provider Omise
items.legal: Legal: We may disclose data if required by Thai law or court order

priceData.title: 4. Price Data and Anonymity
priceData.text: All price history data displayed on Root is fully anonymous. We aggregate sale prices by species and date. Individual transactions, buyer identities, and seller identities are never shown in price graphs. Even logged-in users cannot see who bought or sold at a specific price point.

retention.title: 5. Data Retention
retention.text: We retain your account data as long as you maintain an account. Transaction records are retained for 7 years for tax and legal compliance. If you delete your account, your display name is anonymized in provenance chains but the transaction record itself is preserved as it forms part of plant history.

security.title: 6. Security
security.text: We use industry-standard security measures: Supabase Row-Level Security on all database tables, HTTPS encryption, HMAC-signed QR codes, and webhook signature verification for payment processing. However, no internet transmission is 100% secure.

rights.title: 7. Your Rights
rights.text: Under Thai personal data protection law (PDPA), you have the right to access, correct, delete, and port your personal data. Contact us at privacy@root.market to exercise these rights. We will respond within 30 days.

contact.title: 8. Contact
contact.text: For privacy-related questions, email privacy@root.market or write to Root Plant Market Co., Ltd., 88/19 Soi Sukhumvit 24, Klongton, Klongtoey, Bangkok 10110.

provenance.title: Root Provenance
provenance.description: A permanent, verifiable record of a plant's identity and ownership — a digital birth certificate that travels with the plant for life.

verified.title: Verified identity
verified.desc: Each plant gets a unique QR tag tied to its first listing and photos.

history.title: Full history
history.desc: Every sale and owner is recorded, so the chain of custody is never lost.

fraud.title: Fraud protection
fraud.desc: Buyers can confirm a plant is the one that was actually listed and sold.

why.title: Why provenance matters for plants
why.text: The second-hand and collector plant market runs on trust. A buyer paying a premium for a rare collector plant is trusting the seller's claims about the plant's maturity, stability, and care history — claims that are easy to exaggerate and hard to verify. Provenance replaces "trust me" with a record.

items.0: When a plant is listed, Root generates a unique QR code and ties it to the listing's photos, species, and seller.
items.1: The seller attaches the printed QR tag to the physical plant before shipping.
items.2: Each completed sale appends a new entry to the plant's chain — the new owner, date, and price.
items.3: Anyone scanning the tag sees the plant's full timeline: who grew it, when it changed hands, and for how much.

protects.title: What it protects against
protects.text: Provenance makes it far harder to pass off a different (often lesser) plant than the one advertised, to fake a plant's age or lineage, or to launder a stolen specimen. Sellers build a verifiable reputation through real, recorded sales rather than screenshots.

privacy.title: Ownership & privacy
privacy.text: The chain records pseudonymous seller/buyer handles and transaction metadata — not your home address or payment details. You control what's shown on your public profile.

cta.description: Every plant on Root can carry its own provenance tag.

qrScanner.cameraHint: Point camera at the QR tag on the plant pot
qrScanner.uploadHint: Upload a photo of the QR tag

whatIs.title: What is a Root QR tag?
whatIs.description: Every plant sold on Root gets a unique QR code. Scan it to instantly verify the plant's identity, see its full ownership history, and confirm it came from a trusted seller. It's like a digital birth certificate for your plant.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.text: By accessing or using Root Plant Market ("Root", "we", "us"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, you may not use the platform. Root is a marketplace connecting buyers and sellers of plants within Thailand. We are not a party to any transaction between users.

eligibility.title: 2. Eligibility
eligibility.text: You must be at least 18 years old and resident in Thailand to use Root. By creating an account, you represent that you meet these requirements. Accounts registered by automated methods are not permitted.

account.title: 3. Account Registration
account.text: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials. You agree to notify us immediately of any unauthorized use. Root reserves the right to suspend accounts that violate these terms or receive multiple valid reports from other users.

listing.title: 4. Listing and Selling
listing.intro: When you create a listing on Root, you agree that:

rules.ownership: You are the legal owner of the plant or authorized to sell it
rules.accuracy: The description, photos, and condition are accurate and not misleading
rules.cites: The plant is not illegally collected from wild populations (CITES restrictions apply)
rules.shipWithin48h: You will ship the plant within 48 hours of payment confirmation
rules.attachQr: You will attach the QR provenance tag to the physical plant before shipping

buying.title: 5. Buying
buying.intro: When you purchase a plant on Root, you agree that:

rules.payWithin15m: You will complete payment within 15 minutes of initiating checkout
rules.confirmWithin48h: You will confirm receipt within 48 hours of delivery (or auto-confirm)
rules.livingOrganism: You understand plants are living organisms and some stress from shipping is normal
rules.openDispute: You will open disputes within 48 hours of delivery if there are issues

fees.title: 6. Fees
fees.text: Root charges an 8% platform fee on the sale price, deducted from the seller's payout. There are no listing fees, no monthly fees, and no buyer fees. Payouts are processed to the seller's registered PromptPay ID within 1-2 business days of transaction completion.

prohibited.title: 7. Prohibited Items
prohibited.text: The following may not be listed on Root: illegally collected wild plants (without proper permits), plants restricted under CITES Appendix I, seeds of invasive species, synthetic/chemical plant growth regulators offered for human consumption, and any item that violates Thai law.

disputes.title: 8. Disputes and Refunds
disputes.text: Disputes must be opened within 48 hours of delivery. Root's dispute resolution team will review evidence from both parties. Decisions may result in full refund to buyer, full release to seller, or partial refund. All decisions are final. Root reserves the right to ban users who abuse the dispute system.

provenance.title: 9. QR Provenance
provenance.text: The QR code system is a feature for tracking plant ownership history. Root does not guarantee the accuracy of information entered by users. The QR code remains active even if a user deletes their account, as ownership history is permanent.

liability.title: 10. Limitation of Liability
liability.text: Root is a marketplace platform, not a seller. We are not liable for the quality, safety, or legality of plants listed. Our total liability to you for any claim arising from your use of Root shall not exceed the amount you paid to Root in fees in the 12 months preceding the claim.

governingLaw.title: 11. Governing Law
governingLaw.text: These Terms are governed by the laws of Thailand. Any dispute shall be resolved in the courts of Bangkok.

shippingGuide.title: How to Ship Plants Safely
shippingGuide.subtitle: A step-by-step guide for Thai plant sellers. Follow these 7 steps and your plants will arrive alive, healthy, and happy — every time.

reset.title: Reset Progress?
reset.description: This will clear all checked steps. You cannot undo this.

checklist.title: Packing Checklist
checklist.tip: Tip: Print this page and keep it by your packing station.

gatherMaterials.title: Gather Materials
gatherMaterials.description: Before you touch the plant, make sure you have everything ready. Thai heat means speed matters — you do not want the plant sitting half-packed while you hunt for tape.

details.0: Sphagnum moss (damp, not soaking) — the gold standard for root hydration during transit
details.1: Plastic wrap or zip-lock bags — seal moisture in, keep roots happy
details.2: Rubber bands or twine — secure wraps without crushing stems
details.3: Sturdy cardboard box — plant should not touch any wall; 5cm cushion minimum on all sides
details.4: Packing peanuts, crumpled newspaper, or bubble wrap — fill every gap so nothing shifts
details.5: Strong packing tape — the H-pattern taping method prevents box failure
details.6: "FRAGILE — LIVE PLANTS" labels — make it obvious to handlers
details.7: Bamboo skewers or plant stakes — prevent stem breakage in transit

tips.0: Buy moss in advance from Shopee/Lazada garden shops — it is cheaper than nurseries
tips.1: Save boxes from your own plant deliveries — they are already sized right
tips.2: If shipping aroid cuttings, pre-cut moss squares (10x10cm) to speed up packing

preparePlant.title: Prepare the Plant
preparePlant.description: A well-prepped plant survives 3-day shipping across Thailand. A rushed plant dies in Bangkok traffic. Take your time here.

details.0: Water the plant 2 days before shipping — not the day of. Wet soil + sealed box = root rot.
details.1: Remove dead, yellowing, or damaged leaves — reduces stress and weight
details.2: For bare-root shipping (recommended for aroids, hoyas, orchids):
details.3: – Gently remove the plant from its pot
details.4: – Shake off loose soil, rinse roots gently with room-temperature water
details.5: – Trim any mushy or dead roots with clean scissors
details.6: For potted shipping (cacti, succulents, established plants):
details.7: – Secure soil surface with plastic wrap around the pot
details.8: – Tape the wrap to prevent spillage — Thai couriers are not gentle

tips.0: Take a clear photo of the plant before packing — insurance for disputes
tips.1: For patterned or multicolour plants, mark which side faced the light so buyer knows
tips.2: If roots are long, coil them loosely — do not fold or crimp

protectRoots.title: Protect the Roots
protectRoots.description: Roots are the plant lifeline during shipping. In Thailand's heat, dry roots die in hours. Keep them hydrated but not waterlogged.

details.0: Take a generous handful of damp sphagnum moss — squeeze until a few drops come out
details.1: Wrap the moss completely around the root ball or bare roots
details.2: Seal the moss wrap inside a plastic bag or cling film
details.3: Secure with a rubber band — tight enough to hold, loose enough to not damage
details.4: For potted plants: wrap the entire pot in plastic, tape the top soil layer
details.5: Double-bag if shipping during Songkran or rainy season — humidity is your enemy

tips.0: Add a drop of hydrogen peroxide to the moss water — prevents mold in sealed bags
tips.1: For long transits (south to north Thailand), use slightly more moss
tips.2: Label the bag "ROOTS — KEEP MOIST" so buyer knows not to discard it

securePlant.title: Secure the Plant
securePlant.description: Stems break. Leaves tear. Thai courier vans bounce. Your job is to make the plant immobile inside the box.

details.0: Insert bamboo skewers or stakes into the soil alongside the stem
details.1: Gently tie the stem to the stake with soft plant tie or twine — not wire
details.2: Wrap delicate leaves in soft tissue paper — prevents bruising and drying
details.3: For trailing plants (pothos, hoyas): coil vines loosely and secure with paper loops
details.4: For tall plants: add a second stake crossing the first — creates a cage
details.5: Cover the entire plant with a loose plastic bag (with small air holes) — humidity tent

tips.0: Use biodegradable plant ties — buyers appreciate eco-conscious sellers
tips.1: For velvet-leaf plants (gloriosum, melanochrysum), add extra tissue between leaves
tips.2: If the plant has a new unfurling leaf, support it with a cotton ball wrap

boxItUp.title: Box It Up
boxItUp.description: The box is your plant's home for 1–3 days. Make it a fortress. Shake test is mandatory — if you hear movement, add more padding.

details.0: Choose a box where the plant does not touch any wall — 5cm minimum clearance
details.1: Line the bottom with 5cm of cushioning — packing peanuts or crumpled paper
details.2: Place the wrapped plant in the center — upright, stable, no tilt
details.3: Fill all gaps with packing material — every empty space is a damage risk
details.4: Add a layer of cushioning on top before sealing
details.5: Close the box and do the shake test — no movement sound = perfect
details.6: Seal with H-tape method: tape across the center seam, then tape perpendicular at both ends

tips.0: For expensive plants (5,000+ THB), double-box — inner box with plant, outer box with 3cm gap filled
tips.1: Write "OPEN THIS SIDE" on the correct flap so buyer does not slice the plant
tips.2: Include a silica gel packet if shipping in rainy season — but keep it away from roots

labelSeal.title: Label & Seal
labelSeal.description: Your package will pass through 3–6 handlers who do not care about plants. Labels are the only thing protecting your shipment.

details.0: Write "FRAGILE — LIVE PLANTS" in large letters on all 6 sides of the box
details.1: Add "THIS SIDE UP" arrows — prevents upside-down handling
details.2: Include sender address (your address) and buyer address clearly on top
details.3: Tape over the addresses with clear tape — prevents smudging in rain
details.4: Add a "DO NOT CRUSH" label if the box is lightweight
details.5: Include a small note inside with: your contact, care instructions, and a thank you

tips.0: Use a permanent marker — ballpoint ink smears in Thai humidity
tips.1: Print labels on A4 paper and tape them on — looks more professional than handwriting
tips.2: Add a "Handle with Care" sticker if you have one — visual reinforcement works

chooseCourier.title: Choose Courier & Ship
chooseCourier.description: Thailand has excellent courier options for plants. Pick based on your buyer's location and your budget. Same-day or next-day is always best for live plants.

details.0: Kerry Express — Fast, reliable, handles fragile well. Best for Bangkok metro and major cities.
details.1: Flash Express — Cheapest option, decent for non-fragile plants. Longer transit times.
details.2: J&T Express — Good tracking, wide coverage. Solid middle-ground choice.
details.3: Thailand Post EMS — Best for rural addresses (isan, deep south). Slower but reaches everywhere.
details.4: Grab Express — Same-day for Bangkok/major cities. Expensive but plants arrive fresh.
details.5: Always ship Monday–Thursday — avoids weekend warehouse sitting
details.6: Drop off before 2 PM for same-day pickup by most couriers
details.7: Take a photo of the sealed box with tracking label before handing over

tips.0: Kerry Express has a "Kerry Express Care" option — worth the extra 20 THB for plants
tips.1: Flash Express pickup is free from your home — schedule via their app
tips.2: Always message the buyer the tracking number within 1 hour of drop-off
